stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance in the open-source AI community.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', confirming its primary function as a generative AI model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A zero-day exploit has been discovered that bypasses default BitLocker protections in Windows 11.
The specific mechanism of the exploit is currently unknown, and Microsoft is investigating the vulnerability.
This represents a critical security failure in a major operating system's encryption feature.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges the assumption that LLM backdoors must rely on explicit content-based triggers.
Exploits positional encoding within LLMs as a covert trigger mechanism, posing a new security threat.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective threat coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology synthesizes findings from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to validate benchmark coverage.

stories.title: First public macOS kernel memory corruption exploit on Apple M5
stories.author: quadrige
bullet_points: A public memory corruption exploit targeting the macOS kernel has been discovered.
The exploit specifically affects the Apple M5 chip architecture.
This represents a significant security vulnerability disclosure in Apple's operating system.

stories.title: linux 0-day, access root-owned files as an unprivileged user
stories.author: github.com via dzwdz
bullet_points: A critical zero-day vulnerability has been discovered in the Linux kernel.
The exploit allows an unprivileged user to gain access to files owned by the root user.
This represents a significant security risk requiring immediate attention from system administrators.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline being showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The update is currently in preview, signaling a push for faster, on-device AI experiences.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging apps to local AI agents running entirely on the user's device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI is integrating Codex, its code-writing AI, into the ChatGPT mobile application.
This move aims to compete directly with rivals like Anthropic's Claude Code.
The update represents a push by OpenAI to expand AI functionality across mobile platforms.

stories.title: Energy supplier abandons Lake Tahoe residents to serve data centers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Energy supply in Lake Tahoe is being strained by the demand from large data centers.
Local residents (49,000) are competing for energy resources against data center operations in Nevada.
The story highlights the growing conflict between residential needs and the energy demands of the data center industry.

stories.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: An audit in Ontario revealed significant inaccuracies in AI-powered doctor notetakers.
The AI systems were found to be generating fabricated therapy referrals and incorrect prescriptions.
The story highlights critical reliability and safety concerns regarding the deployment of generative AI in healthcare.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify the stringent safety claims required by modern AI governance frameworks.
Current methodologies (like behavioral evaluations and red-teaming) are epistemically limited to observable model outputs.
Key safety properties demanded by governance, such as absence of hidden objectives, cannot be reliably verified by existing assurance techniques.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework designed to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
The core innovation is encoding new knowledge into a dedicated memory model without altering the original LLM parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, making them more suitable for real-world, evolving applications.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Demonstrates a systematic dual failure where gradient-based unlearning methods lose efficacy under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.
Introduces 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning,' suggesting that current unlearning techniques are fundamentally incompatible with deployed, compressed language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This suggests an enhancement in the capabilities of local LLM tooling for complex, real-time tasks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is expanding functionality on its Ray-Ban Display smart glasses.
The new feature allows users to write messages using only hand gestures.
This capability is being integrated across major messaging platforms like WhatsApp, Messenger, and native Android/iOS messaging.

stories.title: Microsoft starts canceling Claude Code licenses
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft began offering access to Anthropic's Claude Code to its developers in December.
The initial rollout aimed to encourage non-coding employees (like PMs and designers) to experiment with coding.
The news reports that Microsoft has started canceling licenses for Claude Code, suggesting a shift in its adoption strategy.

stories.title: Cisco announces record revenue and 4,000 layoffs in the same day
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Cisco reported record revenue alongside significant workforce reductions.
The company's CFO explicitly stated the layoffs are not a cost-cutting measure.
The news highlights a tension between strong financial performance and corporate restructuring in the enterprise tech sector.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more rigorous evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying chronological sequences of real-world events (e.g., news articles) to ground agent interactions.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their knowledge cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current high-quality 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic, interactive human instructions.
The method moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies used in existing editing techniques.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generative video models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for geometric coherence auditing.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide diagnostic metrics for geometric failures in generated video.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and unstable optimization as bottlenecks when co-training disparate PDE regimes.
Proposes using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands from different physical simulations (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media).

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: OpenDeepThink introduces a new framework to improve LLM reasoning by scaling computation breadth rather than just depth.
It addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best candidate.
This method aims to overcome the noise and bias associated with traditional pointwise LLM judging during inference.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening in medical images.
Addresses limitations in current models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (precise timing).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative richness and temporal accuracy.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are deemed insufficient to verify the stringent safety claims demanded by current AI governance frameworks.
Governance requires verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and resistance to loss-of-control.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are fundamentally limited to observable model outputs.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architectural enhancement that introduces equal conditioning to the decoder.
Aims to improve visual generation quality by preserving structural integrity and reducing detail loss in video generation.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework for testing AI agents in dynamic environments.
The system replays real-world events chronologically to provide realistic testing scenarios for adaptive agents.
Allows agents to forecast future world events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing components in tensor-based models.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Crucial for advancing mechanistic interpretability by allowing researchers to verify if different model parts perform the same computation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically lack of interpretability and historical context referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to improve screening accuracy and transparency.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It proposes a multimodal alignment approach to combine the semantic richness of unstructured text with the temporal precision of structured EHR data.
The application is critical for modeling patient trajectories and forecasting risk in complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models during complex, dexterous robotic manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch when using Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands during human takeover.
The proposed method, 'Hand-in-the-Loop,' aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational challenges of visual reasoning in AI models.
It compares two approaches: computationally expensive direct image generation versus more efficient latent or agentic reasoning.
Agentic methods are noted for incurring context-switching latency due to external tool calls.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new grounded simulation framework for testing AI agents.
The simulation replays real-world events chronologically to mimic dynamic, open-ended environments.
Allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with real-time data streams.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of systematic comparison between retrieval strategy, agent architecture, and tool-calling in agentic search systems.
It focuses on advanced LLM agents capable of autonomous information retrieval, tool usage, and reasoning over large datasets.
The research aims to provide practical insights into optimizing agentic workflows beyond standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured data for better patient trajectory modeling.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static after pretraining, which hinders real-world application in dynamic domains.
Encodes new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model for efficient knowledge integration.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current research where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is currently underrepresented in evaluations.

stories.title: Access to frontier AI will soon be limited by economic and security constraints
stories.author: thoughtpeddler
bullet_points: Access to cutting-edge AI models is facing impending limitations.
These constraints are primarily driven by economic factors.
Security concerns are also cited as a major limiting factor for frontier AI access.

stories.title: UK government replaces Palantir software with internally-built refugee system
stories.author: cdrnsf
bullet_points: The UK government is reportedly replacing Palantir software with a proprietary, internally developed system.
The new system is specifically designed for managing refugee populations.
This indicates a shift towards increased governmental reliance on in-house technology solutions over major external vendors.

stories.title: Old PC vs New AI: Can a 2015 Desktop Actually Run Gemma 4? (2B vs 4B Benchmark)
stories.author: gramli
bullet_points: The article explores the feasibility of running modern AI models (like Gemma 4) on older, legacy hardware (a 2015 desktop).
It focuses on the performance comparison between smaller model sizes (2B vs 4B) to determine local viability.
The topic addresses the growing trend of local, on-device AI inference, even with hardware constraints.
